steps.title: Compare the evidence bases
steps.content: Nair and Shah's smaller study found no effect of environmental knowledge on eco-friendly purchasing, and doubts were raised about how representative that finding was. Chen and colleagues then analyzed 54 studies and found a significant positive effect.

steps.title: Draw the broad implication
steps.content: A makes an unsupported claim about a 202-person sample. C compares Nair and Shah's sample size with most studies, which the text does not do. D compares methodologies, also unsupported. The broader evidence supports doubts about applying Nair and Shah's no-effect conclusion generally. The answer is B.
